examples.title: Basic Unit Testing with pytest
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental unit testing concepts in software quality assurance - writing test cases, assertions, and running tests to verify individual function correctness.
examples.explanation: This example shows basic unit testing where each function is tested in isolation. We create test cases covering normal scenarios, edge cases (0%, 100% discounts), and error conditions. The pytest framework runs all test methods automatically and reports pass/fail status, helping identify bugs early in development.

examples.title: Test-Driven Development (TDD) with Bank Account
examples.description: Demonstrates TDD methodology - writing tests before code, red-green-refactor cycle, and ensuring code meets specified requirements through incremental development.
examples.explanation: TDD follows a cycle: (1) Write failing tests first, (2) Write minimal code to pass tests, (3) Refactor for better design. This ensures every line of code has a corresponding test, leading to better design and fewer bugs. The example shows a BankAccount class developed through tests for deposits, withdrawals, and transfers.

examples.title: Property-Based Testing with Hypothesis
examples.description: Advanced QA technique using Hypothesis library to generate hundreds of random test inputs automatically, verifying properties that should hold true for all valid inputs.
examples.explanation: Property-based testing differs from example-based testing (like unit tests). Instead of writing specific test cases, you define properties that should always hold true. Hypothesis then generates hundreds or thousands of random inputs to test these properties. This catches edge cases that humans often miss. In this example, we test that email validation never crashes, username processing is idempotent, and outputs maintain certain properties across all valid inputs.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of generated examples (default 100)
